meaning: adj.&adv.波浪般的(地) 波状的(地)
examples: These rings are indicative of the crystal structure of nickel and can be explained only by invoking wavelike properties of light.
All radiant energy has wavelike characteristics.
It has to do with the wavelike nature of neutrinos.
The main culprit in the wavelike series of amphibian crashes is a chytrid fungus, known as Bd.
Some trainers teach exercises that involve moving a rope up and down to produce a wavelike motion.
